items.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.
items.owners: andpiccione, Azure/azure-sdk-write-acl, ivarprudnikov, msftsettiy, musabbir, PallabPaul, ryazhang-microsoft, weirenlai

items.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue documents gaps in the `Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger` README, specifically missing documentation for the redirect policy behavior, an unfilled Examples section, and an undocumented `VerifyConnection` default change.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger` (1.4.1-beta.5)
**Affected API:** `ConfidentialLedgerRedirectPolicy`, `ConfidentialLedgerClientOptions.VerifyConnection`
**Scenarios:**
- README `## Examples` section shows "Coming Soon..." instead of real runnable samples
- `ConfidentialLedgerRedirectPolicy` (introduced in 1.4.1-beta.3, improved in 1.4.1-beta.5 via PR #57869) is not documented in the README — users don't know about 307/308 redirect handling and primary-node caching
- `VerifyConnection` default changed from `false` to `true` in beta.4 with no README guidance
- API reference documentation badge link is commented out in the README header

**Root ask:** Update `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/README.md` to document redirect handling, fill the Examples section with real code snippets, and note the `VerifyConnection` default.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation issue filed by an automated workflow triggered by commit `d543f21a7eec10a86152e94f2d19636fb13fcd01`. No runtime bug is present.

The issue body includes a detailed implementation guide (Steps 1–4) with exact markdown to insert into the README and instructions for verifying snippet injection via `Update-Snippets.ps1`.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review PR #57869 to understand the `ConfidentialLedgerRedirectPolicy` caching improvement being documented
2. Implement the README changes in `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/README.md` per the implementation guide in the issue body
3. Check `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/tests/samples/` for existing `#region Snippet:` tags; add them if missing
4. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 confidentialledger` to inject snippet code into README
5. Run `dotnet build` and `dotnet test --filter TestCategory!=Live` to confirm nothing is broken
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — `Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger` is a data-plane client library, not a management (`Azure.ResourceManager.*`) or provisioning library
- **Service:** `Confidential Ledger` — explicitly stated in the issue title, body, and service directory path `sdk/confidentialledger/`
- **Confidence:** High — the package name is explicitly stated, category is unambiguous (data-plane client), and the service label is a direct match to the CODEOWNERS entry
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Confidential Ledger` (line 250) — the issue has "Confidential Ledger" label, satisfying the single-label requirement
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** `@andpiccione` `@Azure/azure-sdk-write-acl` `@ivarprudnikov` `@msftsettiy` `@musabbir` `@PallabPaul` `@ryazhang-microsoft` `@weirenlai`
- **Routing action:** Added "Service Attention" label; posted `@mention` comment to all ServiceOwners; issue left unassigned (no AzureSdkOwners); "needs-team-attention" not added (issue is bot-filed, not customer-reported)
- **Scan notes:** During bottom-to-top scan, `# ServiceLabel: %Confidential Ledger %Mgmt` (line 960) was encountered first but skipped — it requires both "Confidential Ledger" AND "Mgmt", and this issue has "Client" not "Mgmt". Continued upward to line 250 which requires only "Confidential Ledger" → matched.
</details>
